file_name: AudibleInc_20001113_10-Q_EX-10.32_2599586_EX-10.32_Co-Branding Agreement_ Marketing Agreement_ Investment Distribution Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person or entity (including either Party), any other person or entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with that person or entity, or which that person or entity beneficially owns at least fifty percent (50%) of the equity interests therein (provided, however, that with respect to the Parties, no individual or entity as to which the ultimate parent entity of a Party does not directly or indirectly control the operations or management thereof (e.g., in the case of ACSI as of the date of this Agreement, Gear.com, Inc.) shall be deemed to be an Affiliate of such Party for purposes of this Agreement).
perturbation.changed_text: "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person or entity (including either Party), any other person or entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with that person or entity, or which that person or entity beneficially owns at least ten percent (10%) of the equity interests therein (provided, however, that with respect to the Parties, no individual or entity as to which the ultimate parent entity of a Party does not directly or indirectly control the operations or management thereof (e.g., in the case of ACSI as of the date of this Agreement, Gear.com, Inc.) shall be deemed to be an Affiliate of such Party for purposes of this Agreement).
perturbation.explanation: The change in the definition of 'Affiliate', specifically altering the equity interest threshold from 'at least fifty percent (50%)' to 'at least ten percent (10%)', creates an in-text contradiction. This is because the definition of affiliate is being changed to something that is less constricting. This change affects Section 6.2 and 6.3 [ACSI License & Company License] due to the right to sublicense to affiliates and now its less constricted because the definition of affiliates has changed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ACSI hereby grants to Company, during the Term, a non- exclusive, non-transferable (except in accordance with Section 11.7 [Assignment]) license, which Company may sublicense only to its Affiliates, to use the ACSI Intellectual Property supplied by ACSI to Company as is reasonably necessary to perform its obligations under this Agreement; provided, however, that Company shall not use ACSI's Trademarks, including in any advertising, without ACSI's prior written consent, unless such use conforms to a written Trademark use policy previously furnished by ACSI to Company and not subsequently modified or revoked. All goodwill arising out of any use of any of ACSI's or its Affiliates' Trademarks by, through or under Company will inure solely to the benefit of ACSI and its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Affiliate' as owning at least 50% equity interests. The changed text lowers this threshold to 10%. Section 6.2 grants the Company a license to sublicense the ACSI Intellectual Property only to its Affiliates. By lowering the threshold to 10%, the definition of Affiliate becomes broader, allowing Company to sublicense ACSI Intellectual Property to a larger group of entities that are now considered 'Affiliates' under the new definition. This directly expands the scope of the sublicense rights granted to the Company, which can be argued to contradict the original intent or potentially introduce risk ACSI did not agree to, creating an in-text contradiction that falls under Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: "Confidential Information" means, with respect to either Party, all information relating to such Party or its Affiliates that is designated as confidential or that, given the nature of the information or the circumstances surrounding its disclosure, reasonably should -3- be considered as confidential. Confidential Information includes, without limitation, (a) all information relating to a Party's or its Affiliates' technology, customers, business plans, promotional and marketing activities, finances and other business affairs; (b) all third party information that a Party or its Affiliates is obligated to keep confidential; and (c) the terms of this Agreement. Confidential Information may be contained in tangible materials, such as drawings, data, specifications, reports and computer programs, or may be in the nature of unwritten knowledge. Confidential Information does not include any information that (i) has become publicly available without breach of this Agreement, (ii) can be shown by documentation to have been known to the Receiving Party at the time of its receipt from the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates without a breach of confidentiality or other improper means, (iii) is received from a third party who did not acquire or disclose such information by a wrongful or tortious act or (iv) can be shown by documentation to have been independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential Information" means, with respect to either Party, all information relating to such Party or its Affiliates that is designated as confidential or that, given the nature of the information or the circumstances surrounding its disclosure, reasonably should -3- be considered as confidential. Confidential Information includes, without limitation, (a) all information relating to a Party's or its Affiliates' technology, customers, business plans, promotional and marketing activities, finances and other business affairs; (b) all third party information that a Party or its Affiliates is obligated to keep confidential; and (c) the terms of this Agreement. Confidential Information may be contained in tangible materials, such as drawings, data, specifications, reports and computer programs, or may be in the nature of unwritten knowledge. Confidential Information includes any information that (i) has become publicly available without breach of this Agreement, (ii) can be shown by documentation to have been known to the Receiving Party at the time of its receipt from the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates without a breach of confidentiality or other improper means, (iii) is received from a third party who did not acquire or disclose such information by a wrongful or tortious act or (iv) can be shown by documentation to have been independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information does not include' to 'Confidential Information includes' for publicly available information, previously known information, information from third parties, and independently developed information the contract becomes contradictory. Section 11.4 [Nondisclosure] is contradicted, which now obligates them to protect even information that is publicly available, previously known, acquired from third parties, or independently developed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party will protect the Confidential Information of the other Party from misappropriation and unauthorized use or disclosure, and at a minimum, will take precautions at least as great as those taken to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event with less than reasonable care. Without limiting the foregoing, the Receiving Party will: (a) use such Confidential Information solely for the purposes for which it has been disclosed; and (b) disclose such Confidential Information only to those of its and its Affiliates' employees, agents, and consultants who have a need to know the same for the purpose of performing this Agreement and who are informed of and agree to a duty of nondisclosure. The Receiving Party may also disclose Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to the extent necessary to comply with applicable Law or legal process or pursuant to a registration report or exhibits thereto filed or to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission, listing agency or any stock securities commission, or any other associated filings, provided that the Receiving Party uses gives the Disclosing Party not less than ten (10) days' prior written notice thereof and complies with all reasonable requests of the Disclosing Party to minimize the extent or scope of any such disclosure. Upon request of the other Party, or in any event upon any termination or expiration of the Term, each Party shall return to the other or destroy (and certify in writing such destruction) all materials, in any medium, which contain, embody, reflect or Source: AUDIBLE INC, 10-Q, 11/13/2000 reference all or any part of any Confidential Information of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes certain information from being considered Confidential Information (i.e., publicly available, previously known, third-party, and independently developed information). The changed text removes this exclusion, now including this information under the definition of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts Section 11.4 [Nondisclosure], which obligates each party to protect the other party's Confidential Information. The modified definition now forces them to protect information that was previously explicitly stated to be outside the scope of protection, creating a clear and direct contradiction. The change fundamentally alters the scope of confidentiality obligations, rendering the contract internally inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. The contradiction is strong because it imposes an obligation to protect information previously excluded from protection.

perturbation.original_text: "Term" means the term of this Agreement as defined in Section 10.
perturbation.changed_text: "Term" means the term of this Agreement shall be indefinite regardless of what is defined in Section 10.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Term' to indicate the agreement's duration is indefinite, a contradiction arises with Section 10, which specifies a finite term. Section 10.1 [Term] states that the agreement 'will end automatically upon the end of Year 3'. Making 'Term' indefinite causes confusion about the agreement's actual duration and enforceability, specifically related to renewal, and the end date and all other term durations found within the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement will commence on the Effective Date, and unless earlier terminated as provided elsewhere in this Agreement, will end automatically upon the end of Year 3.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Term' to be indefinite, directly contradicting the contradicted text which states the agreement will end automatically after Year 3. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as to the duration of the agreement. The explanation provided also reinforces that the changed text is in direct contradiction with the contradicted text which specified an end date. This contradiction directly impacts enforceability and understanding of the contract's duration.
